'''Sonny Nickle''' (born 4 May 1969) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Sonny Nickle was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England. He is the youngest of four brothers.

==Playing career==
Nickle joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from [[Sheffield Eagles]] in July 1991. St Helens paid Sheffield an initial fee of £80,000, set by an independent tribunal, and an additional £25,000 after Nickle was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Nickle Test worth a few dimes |url=https://www.proquest.com/docview/187512875/CA8BA84EB48E4AC6PQ |work=The Guardian |date=27 October 1993 |page=15 |url-access=subscription |id={{ProQuest|187512875}} }}</ref>

Sonny Nickle played {{rlp|sr}} in St Helens 4–5 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Sunday 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=13 July 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 1995, he was part of the package put together by St Helens to bring [[Paul Newlove]] to the club from the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Sonny Nickle was an athletic [[Rugby league positions#Forwards|forward]] who was a powerful runner. He enjoyed the distinction of representing Great Britain on six occasions.

Nickle played for Bradford at {{rlp|sr}} in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat by St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-date=8 August 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Nickle returned to St Helens in 1999, and played in their [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Bradford Bulls]]. Having won the 1999 Championship, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] contested in the [[2000 World Club Challenge]] against [[National Rugby League]] Premiers the [[Melbourne Storm]], with Nickle playing at {{rlp|hk}} in the loss. Nickle appeared from the bench in the Saints' [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Wigan Warriors]].  As [[Super League V]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]], Nickle played at {{rlp|pr}} in Saints' victory.

In September 2001, Nickle was suspended for six months for a tackle on [[Leeds Rhinos]] hooker [[Robbie Mears]], which broke the Leeds player's jaw.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Six-month ban may cost Nickle his Saints future |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/sixmonth-ban-may-cost-nickle-his-saints-future-9201061.html |website=The Independent |access-date=27 August 2022 |date=12 September 2001}}</ref> The suspension was changed to a nine-match ban following an appeal.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Nickle wins longer ban |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2001/sep/19/rugbyleague.sthelens |website=The Guardian |access-date=27 August 2022 |date=19 September 2001}}</ref> The club attempted to shorten the ban by allowing Nickle to sign a short-term contract with [[Northern Ford Premiership]] side [[Barrow Border Raiders]], whose new season started earlier than St Helens.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Plummer |first1=David |title=Nickle serves ban the easy way |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2001/nov/29/rugbyleague.sthelens |website=The Guardian |access-date=27 August 2022 |date=29 November 2001}}</ref> When Nickle re-signed with Saints, the RFL confirmed that Nickle had to serve the remainder of his ban, as the matches with Barrow would not count towards his suspension.<ref>{{cite web |title=Nickle must serve ban |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1733111.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=27 August 2022 |date=29 December 2001}}</ref>
